summary: Device policy for turning Program Compatibility Assistant (PCA) off. The correct registry value is DisablePCA under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat, documented by Microsoft Learn ADMX_AppCompat and local AppCompat.admx. The app implementation was corrected on 2026-03-13 to use DisablePCA=1 instead of the previously mismatched DisablePcaUI diagnostics policy, and Procmon confirmed the runtime write.

setting.name: Disable Program Compatibility Assistant
setting.casual_explanation: Program Compatibility Assistant monitors applications and can suggest compatibility solutions when Windows detects install failures, runtime failures, or blocked drivers. This policy turns PCA off.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn PCA off when they want fewer compatibility prompts on systems that run only current software. They leave it on when they regularly run older apps or drivers that may need Windows compatibility help.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning PCA off removes a Windows fallback that can detect application and driver compatibility problems, then offer compatibility-mode or online-help suggestions. On a modern system that runs only current software the practical impact may be low, but the behavior is still different from the default.
setting.professional_notes: The official device policy is AppCompatTurnOffProgramCompatibilityAssistant_2 under AppCompat.admx. Microsoft Learn ADMX_AppCompat documents Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat and Registry Value Name DisablePCA for that policy. Local AppCompat.admx maps enabledValue=1 and disabledValue=0, and local AppCompat.adml states that enabling the policy turns PCA off while disabling or not configuring it leaves PCA on. Procmon capture on 2026-03-13 confirmed svchost.exe -k GPSvcGroup writes DisablePCA=1 during policy application on Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200.8037.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps Program Compatibility Assistant turned on. PCA can monitor applications and offer compatibility solutions when problems are detected.

allowed_values.label: Turn off Program Compatibility Assistant
allowed_values.meaning: enabledValue in AppCompat.admx. PCA is turned off, so Windows will not present compatibility solutions for detected application issues. Procmon confirmed svchost.exe (gpsvc) writes DisablePCA=REG_DWORD 0x1 during gpupdate /force on Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200.8037 (2026-03-13).

allowed_values.label: PCA enabled (policy explicit)
allowed_values.meaning: disabledValue in AppCompat.admx. Equivalent to missing: PCA remains turned on.

targets.notes: Windows default is missing (key absent), which behaves like value=0: Program Compatibility Assistant stays on.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app now writes DisablePCA=1 under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat, which matches the documented machine policy for turning Program Compatibility Assistant off.

windows_defaults.label: Windows managed default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows editions where Program Compatibility Assistant is present

states.rationale: Policy key is absent. Program Compatibility Assistant remains turned on in its default state.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows managed default
intended_for: Systems that run legacy or mixed-age software
avoid_for: People who explicitly want PCA turned off

states.rationale: Leave PCA policy unset. Windows keeps Program Compatibility Assistant turned on.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to offer compatibility help when older apps or drivers have problems.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to suppress PCA entirely on a modern-only system.
tradeoffs: PCA remains active for application and driver compatibility problems.
No registry key is written; fully reversible by definition.

recommended_profiles.label: Turn off Program Compatibility Assistant
intended_for: Advanced users on modern systems running only current software
avoid_for: Systems that regularly run older applications or drivers needing compatibility help
General users who may benefit from compatibility prompts

states.rationale: Enable the documented policy that turns Program Compatibility Assistant off.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a quieter system and already know you do not rely on PCA suggestions.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You use legacy software or want Windows to surface compatibility solutions automatically.
tradeoffs: PCA compatibility prompts and guidance are turned off.
Older applications may no longer trigger helpful compatibility suggestions.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AppCompat.admx mapping for Turn off Program Compatibility Assistant
evidence.summary: Documents AppCompatTurnOffProgramCompatibilityAssistant_2 under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat with value name DisablePCA, enabledValue=1, and disabledValue=0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AppCompat.adml help text for Turn off Program Compatibility Assistant
evidence.summary: States that enabling the policy turns Program Compatibility Assistant off and disabling or not configuring it leaves PCA on. Also notes that PCA diagnostics settings are configured separately.

evidence.title: Policy CSP - ADMX_AppCompat (Turn off Program Compatibility Assistant)
evidence.summary: Microsoft Learn documents AppCompatTurnOffProgramCompatibilityAssistant_2 as the device policy named 'Turn off Program Compatibility Assistant' with Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat and Registry Value Name DisablePCA.

evidence.title: Procmon + LGPO capture - Turn off Program Compatibility Assistant
evidence.summary: LGPO.exe applied DisablePCA=1 via local policy. Procmon captured svchost.exe -k GPSvcGroup (gpsvc) writing HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat\DisablePCA=REG_DWORD 0x1 during gpupdate /force. reg query confirmed the value at the expected path. Rollback removed Registry.pol and gpt.ini, deleted DisablePCA, and gpupdate /force returned the key to its baseline state. Capture file: C:\Users\Deniz\AppData\Local\Temp\disable_pca_capture_20260313_153643.pml

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: Current implementation writes DisablePCA=1 under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat for the tweak named 'Disable Program Compatibility Assistant'.

decision.why: Microsoft Learn explicitly documents DisablePCA under the AppCompat policy CSP, local AppCompat.admx confirms the 1/0 mapping, the app now writes the same value, and Procmon captured gpsvc applying DisablePCA=1 at the expected machine-policy path.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Friendly Name Turn off Program Compatibility Assistant; Registry Key Name Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\AppCompat; Registry Value Name DisablePCA
validation_proof.notes: The official Microsoft Learn ADMX_AppCompat page names the same registry key and value name that the corrected app implementation now writes.
